title: Danny Strong and the Recipe for a Classic Biopic | The Big Picture (Ep. 25)
format: Sean Fennessey and Chris Ryan try to define the biopic through their favorite examples, then Fennessey talks to writer-director Danny Strong about his J.D. Salinger film Rebel in the Rye.

segments.heading: What makes a biopic
segments.note: Sean Fennessey and Chris Ryan on the form

films.title: Malcolm X
films.note: Ryan's favorite biopic and, to him, Spike Lee's Citizen Kane; an act of subjective biography carried by Denzel Washington.

films.title: Citizen Kane
films.note: Held up as the stylized end of the spectrum against Malcolm X's fictionalized one.

films.title: Ray
films.note: The default template: name in the title, childhood trauma, rise-fall-rise across a whole life.

films.title: Straight Outta Compton
films.note: A hit that captures only a narrow slice of its subjects' lives.

films.title: Raging Bull
films.note: The opposite scope: roughly a 40-year span of one man.

films.title: The Imitation Game
films.note: Reads more like a procedural than a biography: the task, not the character.

films.title: Steve Jobs
films.note: Skips the usual expository life story and stages the man across three product launches; a Sorkin favorite of both hosts.

films.title: Molly's Game
films.note: Named as the next Sorkin, still to come.

films.title: The Assassination of Jesse James by the Coward Robert Ford
films.note: A film about the mythology of a person, told through someone else's eyes.

films.title: Wyatt Earp
films.note: Ryan's soft spot: Costner's life's-work Western, admired for how unconcerned it is with being cool; overshadowed by Tombstone.

films.title: Tombstone
films.note: The quotable, rewatchable counterpart with Val Kilmer.

films.title: Amadeus
films.note: Forman's portrait of a fascinating but not good man: Mozart as a frothy fool at war with Salieri.

films.title: The People vs. Larry Flynt
films.note: Same director, Milos Forman, drawn to grand eccentrics.

films.title: Ed Wood
films.note: Ryan's number-one example of loving portrayal: Burton and Depp make a failure the hero; the last good Burton movie.

films.title: Lincoln
films.note: Great filmmaking that mostly confirms what we already knew about its subject.

films.title: Lawrence of Arabia
films.note: A perfect movie Ryan struggles to place: is it about the man or the grandeur of widescreen cinema?

films.title: 24 Hour Party People
films.note: Ryan's pick: Tony Wilson riding the post-punk and Manchester dance music wave.

films.title: Milk
films.note: The kindred film: a subject caught in the middle of a great cultural upheaval.

segments.heading: Danny Strong on Rebel in the Rye

films.title: Rebel in the Rye
films.note: His directorial debut, on the middle stretch of J.D. Salinger's life: the war, the trauma, the writing of Catcher in the Rye. He optioned the biography himself, wrote on spec, and recut significantly after Sundance.

films.title: Salinger
films.note: The documentary he feared would make his film irrelevant; it turned out birth-to-death and completely different from his period piece.

films.title: The Best of Enemies
films.note: He's producing it: Taraji P. Henson and Sam Rockwell, Robin Bissell's directing debut; the first cut made him cry.

films.title: Battle of the Sexes
films.note: Caught a screening and expects people to like it.

films.title: Seabiscuit
films.note: Bissell's producing credit, part of why Strong came aboard The Best of Enemies.

films.title: The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 1
films.note: One of the studio assignments that kept pushing the Salinger movie back.

films.title: The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 2
films.note: After finishing it he turned down about ten offers to go write Rebel in the Rye.

films: King Arthur: Legend of the Sword

The Assassination of Jesse James by the.title: The Assassination of Jesse James by the Coward Robert Ford

King Arthur: Legend of the Sword.title: King Arthur: Legend of the Sword
